metadata.name: COBIT 2019
metadata.description: COBIT 2019 (Control Objectives for Information and Related Technologies) is ISACA's comprehensive framework for the governance and management of enterprise information and technology (I&T). It provides a structured approach to align IT strategy with business objectives, manage risk, optimize resources, and deliver value through effective IT governance.
metadata.classification_reasoning: COBIT 2019 is a governance and management framework providing principles, practices, and analytical tools for enterprise I&T governance. It is used as a reference framework rather than a certification standard, though it supports CGEIT and CISA certifications.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide a comprehensive framework for governance and management of enterprise information and technology, enabling organizations to achieve their strategic objectives through effective use of IT resources while managing risk and ensuring compliance.
metadata.framework_scope: All aspects of enterprise information and technology governance and management, applicable to organizations of all sizes and industries.
framework_objectives: Ensure stakeholder value delivery through effective IT governance
Optimize risk management and resource utilization
Align IT strategy with business objectives
metadata.target_audience: Board of Directors, C-Suite Executives, IT Governance Professionals, IT Auditors, Risk Managers, Compliance Officers, CIOs, CISOs, IT Managers
adoption_approach: Understand the enterprise context and strategy
Determine initial scope using COBIT design factors
Assess current capability levels using the COBIT Performance Management model
Set target capability levels based on enterprise needs
Develop improvement roadmap and governance system design
Implement governance components and monitor performance

controls.title: Ensured Governance Framework Setting and Maintenance
controls.description: Analyze and articulate the requirements for the governance of enterprise I&T. Put in place and maintain the governance enablers with agreed-on principles, decision-making models, and authority levels. Establish the governance structures and processes in line with the design factors.
controls.full_text: EDM01 — Ensured Governance Framework Setting and Maintenance. Purpose: Provide a consistent approach integrated and aligned with the enterprise governance approach. I&T-related decisions are made in line with the enterprise's strategies and objectives, and desired value is realized effectively. To that end, ensure that I&T-related processes are overseen effectively and transparently; compliance with legal, contractual, and regulatory requirements is confirmed; and the governance requirements for board members are met. Practices include: EDM01.01 Evaluate the governance system — Continually identify and engage with the enterprise's stakeholders, document an understanding of the requirements, and make a judgment on the current and future design of governance of enterprise I&T. EDM01.02 Direct the governance system — Inform leaders and obtain their support, buy-in, and commitment. Guide the structures, processes, and practices for the governance of I&T in line with agreed-on governance design principles, decision-making models, and authority levels. Define the information required for informed decision making. EDM01.03 Monitor the governance system — Monitor the effectiveness and performance of the enterprise's governance of I&T. Assess whether the governance system and implemented mechanisms (including structures, principles, and processes) are operating effectively and provide appropriate oversight of I&T.
evidence_requirements: IT Governance Charter and framework documentation
Governance structure organizational charts with roles and responsibilities
Board meeting minutes documenting IT governance decisions
Periodic governance effectiveness review reports

controls.title: Ensured Benefits Delivery
controls.description: Optimize the value contribution to the business from the business processes, IT services, and IT assets resulting from investment made by IT at an acceptable cost. Secure the required capabilities for delivering the optimal value from I&T-enabled initiatives.
controls.full_text: EDM02 — Ensured Benefits Delivery. Purpose: Secure optimal value from I&T-enabled initiatives, services, and assets; cost-efficient delivery of solutions and services; and a reliable and accurate picture of costs and likely benefits so that business needs are supported effectively and efficiently. Practices include: EDM02.01 Evaluate value optimization — Continually evaluate the portfolio of I&T-enabled investments, services, and assets to determine the likelihood of achieving enterprise objectives and delivering value at a reasonable cost. Identify and make judgment on any changes in direction that need to be given to management to optimize value creation. EDM02.02 Direct value optimization — Direct value management principles and practices to enable optimal value realization from I&T-enabled investments throughout their full economic life cycle. EDM02.03 Monitor value optimization — Monitor the key goals and metrics to determine the extent to which the business is generating the expected value and benefits to the enterprise from I&T-enabled investments and services. Identify significant issues and consider corrective actions.
evidence_requirements: IT investment portfolio with business case documentation
Benefits realization tracking reports and dashboards
Value optimization metrics and KPI reports
Post-implementation reviews of IT-enabled initiatives

controls.title: Ensured Risk Optimization
controls.description: Ensure that I&T-related enterprise risk does not exceed the enterprise's risk appetite and risk tolerance, the impact of I&T risk to enterprise value is identified and managed, and the potential for compliance failures is minimized.
controls.full_text: EDM03 — Ensured Risk Optimization. Purpose: Ensure that I&T-related enterprise risk does not exceed risk appetite and risk tolerance, the impact of I&T risk to enterprise value is identified and managed, and the potential for compliance failures is minimized. Practices include: EDM03.01 Evaluate risk management — Continually examine and make judgment on the effect of risk on the current and future use of I&T in the enterprise. Consider whether the enterprise's risk appetite is appropriate and that risk to enterprise value related to the use of I&T is identified and managed. EDM03.02 Direct risk management — Direct the establishment of risk management practices to provide reasonable assurance that I&T risk management practices are appropriate to ensure that the actual I&T risk does not exceed the board's risk appetite. EDM03.03 Monitor risk management — Monitor the key goals and metrics of the risk management processes and establish how deviations or problems will be identified, tracked, and reported for remediation.
evidence_requirements: Enterprise risk appetite and tolerance statements
IT risk register with risk assessments and treatment plans
Risk monitoring dashboards and trend reports
Risk committee meeting minutes and escalation records

controls.title: Ensured Resource Optimization
controls.description: Ensure that adequate and sufficient I&T-related capabilities (people, process, and technology) are available to support enterprise objectives effectively at optimal cost.
controls.full_text: EDM04 — Ensured Resource Optimization. Purpose: Ensure that the resource needs of the enterprise are met in the optimal manner, I&T costs are optimized, and there is an increased likelihood of benefit realization and readiness for future change. Practices include: EDM04.01 Evaluate resource management — Continually examine and make judgment on the current and future need for I&T-related resources, options for resourcing (including sourcing strategies), and allocation and management principles to meet the needs of the enterprise in the optimal manner. EDM04.02 Direct resource management — Ensure the adoption of resource management principles to enable optimal use of I&T resources throughout their full economic life cycle. EDM04.03 Monitor resource management — Monitor the key goals and metrics of the resource management processes and establish how deviations or problems will be identified, tracked, and reported for remediation.
evidence_requirements: IT resource allocation plans and capacity assessments
IT budget and cost optimization reports
Sourcing strategy documentation and vendor assessments
Resource utilization metrics and workforce planning reports

controls.title: Ensured Stakeholder Engagement
controls.description: Ensure that stakeholders are supportive of the I&T strategy and roadmap, communication to stakeholders is effective and timely, and the basis for reporting is established to increase performance.
controls.full_text: EDM05 — Ensured Stakeholder Engagement. Purpose: Ensure that stakeholders are supportive of the I&T strategy and roadmap, communication to stakeholders is effective and timely, and the basis for reporting is established to increase performance. Identify areas for improvement, and confirm that I&T-related objectives and strategies are aligned with the enterprise's strategy. Practices include: EDM05.01 Evaluate stakeholder reporting requirements — Continually examine and make judgment on the current and future requirements for stakeholder communication and reporting, including both mandatory reporting requirements and communication to other stakeholders. Establish the principles for communication. EDM05.02 Direct stakeholder communication and reporting — Ensure the establishment of effective stakeholder communication and reporting, including mechanisms to ensure the quality and completeness of information, oversight of mandatory reporting, and creating a communication strategy for stakeholders. EDM05.03 Monitor stakeholder engagement — Monitor the effectiveness of stakeholder communication. Assess mechanisms for ensuring accuracy, reliability, and effectiveness and ascertain whether requirements of different stakeholders are met.
evidence_requirements: Stakeholder communication strategy and plans
IT governance reporting templates and dashboards
Stakeholder satisfaction survey results
Board and management reporting packages for IT

controls.title: Managed I&T Management Framework
controls.description: Implement and maintain mechanisms and authorities to manage information and technology in the enterprise in line with the overall governance approach and design factors. Establish organizational structures that reflect business needs and IT priorities.
controls.full_text: APO01 — Managed I&T Management Framework. Purpose: Provide a consistent management approach to enable the enterprise governance requirements to be met, covering management processes, organizational structures, roles and responsibilities, reliable and repeatable activities, and information items. Practices include: APO01.01 Design the management system for enterprise I&T — Design the management system based on the enterprise context and design factors. APO01.02 Communicate management objectives and direction — Communicate I&T management awareness and direction to all relevant stakeholders. APO01.03 Define organizational structures — Establish internal and external organizational structures that reflect business needs and IT priorities. APO01.04 Define roles, responsibilities, and authorities — Establish clear roles, responsibilities, and authority levels. APO01.05 Define the target capability levels — Based on the enterprise context, define appropriate capability targets for priority processes. APO01.06 Manage continual improvement of the management system — Assess management system performance and improvements continually.
evidence_requirements: IT management framework documentation and policies
Organizational structure charts with IT roles and responsibilities
RACI matrices for IT processes
Capability maturity assessments and improvement plans

controls.title: Managed Strategy
controls.description: Provide a holistic view of the current business and IT environment, the future direction, and the initiatives required to migrate to the desired future environment. Leverage enterprise architecture building blocks and consider long-term implications.
controls.full_text: APO02 — Managed Strategy. Purpose: Support the digital transformation strategy of the organization and deliver the desired value through a roadmap of incremental changes. Leverage enterprise architecture building blocks and components, including externally available services, to create new capabilities and leverage current capabilities and I&T innovations to create new business and IT opportunities. Practices include: APO02.01 Understand enterprise direction — Consider the current enterprise environment and business processes, as well as the enterprise strategy and future objectives, including the external environment of the enterprise. APO02.02 Assess the current environment, capabilities, and performance — Assess the performance of current internal business and I&T capabilities and external I&T services, and develop an understanding of the enterprise architecture in relation to I&T. APO02.03 Define the target I&T capabilities — Define the target business and I&T capabilities and required I&T services. APO02.04 Conduct a gap analysis — Identify the gaps between the current and target environments and consider alignment. APO02.05 Define the strategic plan and roadmap — Create a strategic plan that defines, in cooperation with relevant stakeholders, how I&T-related goals will contribute to the enterprise's strategic goals. APO02.06 Communicate the I&T strategy and direction — Create awareness and understanding of the business and I&T objectives and direction, as captured in the I&T strategy, through communication to appropriate stakeholders.
evidence_requirements: IT strategic plan aligned with business strategy
Current state and target state architecture documentation
Gap analysis reports with prioritized initiatives
IT strategy communication and roadmap presentations

controls.title: Managed Enterprise Architecture
controls.description: Establish a common architecture consisting of business process, information, data, application, and technology architecture layers for effectively and efficiently realizing enterprise and IT strategies.
controls.full_text: APO03 — Managed Enterprise Architecture. Purpose: Represent the different building blocks that make up the enterprise and their interrelationships as well as the principles guiding their design and evolution over time, enabling a standard, responsive, and efficient delivery of operational and strategic objectives. Practices include: APO03.01 Develop the enterprise architecture vision — The architecture vision provides a first-cut, high-level description of the baseline and target architectures, covering the business, information, data, application, and technology domains. APO03.02 Define reference architecture — The reference architecture describes the current and target architectures for the business, information, data, application, and technology domains. APO03.03 Select opportunities and solutions — Rationalize the gaps between baseline and target architectures, consider both the technical perspective and a broad enterprise-wide viewpoint, and group them into solution building blocks. APO03.04 Define architecture implementation — Create a viable architecture implementation and migration plan in alignment with the solution and project portfolios. APO03.05 Provide enterprise architecture services — Provide enterprise architecture services within the enterprise, including guidance and monitoring of implementation projects, formalizing ways of working through architecture contracts, measuring and communicating value added.
evidence_requirements: Enterprise architecture documentation (business, data, application, technology layers)
Architecture principles and standards documentation
Architecture review board meeting minutes and decisions
Architecture roadmap and transition plans

controls.title: Managed Innovation
controls.description: Achieve competitive advantage, business innovation, and improved operational effectiveness and efficiency by exploiting I&T developments and emerging technologies.
controls.full_text: APO04 — Managed Innovation. Purpose: Achieve competitive advantage, business innovation, improved customer experience/satisfaction and improved operational effectiveness and efficiency by exploiting I&T developments and emerging technologies. Practices include: APO04.01 Create an environment conducive to innovation — Create an environment that is conducive to innovation, considering methods such as culture, rewards, collaboration, technology forums, and mechanisms to promote and capture employee ideas. APO04.02 Maintain an understanding of the enterprise environment — Work with relevant stakeholders to understand their challenges. Maintain an adequate understanding of enterprise strategy and the competitive environment or other constraints so that opportunities enabled by new technologies can be identified. APO04.03 Monitor and scan the technology environment — Perform systematic monitoring and scanning of the enterprise's external environment to identify emerging technologies that have the potential to create value. APO04.04 Assess the potential of emerging technologies and innovative ideas — Analyze identified emerging technologies and other I&T innovation ideas. APO04.05 Recommend appropriate further initiatives — Evaluate and monitor the results of proof-of-concept initiatives and, if favorable, generate recommendations for further initiatives and gain stakeholder support.
evidence_requirements: Innovation strategy and program documentation
Technology watch and emerging technology assessment reports
Proof-of-concept evaluation results and business cases
Innovation portfolio tracking and benefits reports

controls.title: Managed Portfolio
controls.description: Execute the strategic direction set for investments in line with the enterprise architecture vision and the desired characteristics of the investment portfolio. Consider the different categories of investments and the resources and funding constraints.
controls.full_text: APO05 — Managed Portfolio. Purpose: Optimize the performance of the overall portfolio of programs in response to individual program and service performance and changing enterprise priorities and demands. Practices include: APO05.01 Establish the target investment mix — Review and ensure clarity on enterprise strategies and current IT priorities. Define an appropriate investment mix based on cost, alignment with strategy, and financial measures such as cost and expected ROI. APO05.02 Determine the availability and sources of funds — Determine potential sources of funds, different funding options, and implications of the funding source on the investment return expectations. APO05.03 Evaluate and select programs to fund — Based on the requirements of the overall investment portfolio mix, evaluate, prioritize, and balance programs and services against each other. APO05.04 Monitor, optimize, and report on investment portfolio performance — On a regular basis, monitor and optimize the performance of the investment portfolio and individual programs throughout the investment full economic life cycle. APO05.05 Maintain portfolios — Maintain portfolios of programs and projects, products and services, and I&T assets.
evidence_requirements: IT investment portfolio documentation and prioritization criteria
Program and project portfolio status reports
Portfolio performance metrics and ROI analysis
Investment decision records and funding allocation documentation

controls.title: Managed Budget and Costs
controls.description: Manage the I&T-related financial activities in both the business and IT functions, covering budget, cost and benefit management, and prioritization of spending through formal budgeting practices.
controls.full_text: APO06 — Managed Budget and Costs. Purpose: Foster partnership between IT and enterprise stakeholders to enable the effective and efficient use of I&T-related resources and provide transparency and accountability of the cost and business value of solutions and services. Enable the enterprise to make informed decisions regarding the use of I&T solutions and services. Practices include: APO06.01 Manage finance and accounting — Establish and maintain a method for accounting for all I&T-related costs, investments, and depreciation as integral part of the enterprise's financial systems and chart of accounts. APO06.02 Prioritize resource allocation — Implement a decision-making process to prioritize the allocation of resources and rules for discretionary investments by individual business units. APO06.03 Create and maintain budgets — Prepare a budget reflecting the investment priorities supporting the strategic objectives based on the portfolio of I&T-enabled programs and I&T services. APO06.04 Model and allocate costs — Establish and use an I&T costing model based on the service definition, ensuring that allocation of costs for services is identifiable, measurable, and predictable. APO06.05 Manage costs — Implement a cost management process comparing actual costs to budgets. Costs should be monitored and reported and deviations timely identified and their impact assessed.
evidence_requirements: IT budget documentation and approval records
Cost allocation models and chargeback/showback reports
Budget variance analysis and cost management reports
IT financial management policies and procedures

controls.title: Managed Human Resources
controls.description: Provide a structured approach to ensure optimal structuring, placement, decision rights, and skills of human resources. This includes communicating the defined roles and responsibilities, learning and growth plans, and performance expectations.
controls.full_text: APO07 — Managed Human Resources. Purpose: Optimize the human resources capabilities to meet enterprise objectives. Practices include: APO07.01 Maintain adequate and appropriate staffing — Evaluate staffing requirements on a regular basis or upon major changes to the enterprise or operational or IT environments to ensure that the enterprise has sufficient human resources to support enterprise goals and objectives. APO07.02 Identify key IT personnel — Identify key IT personnel and minimize reliance on a single individual performing a critical job function through knowledge capture (documentation), knowledge sharing, succession planning, and staff backup. APO07.03 Maintain the skills and competencies of personnel — Define and manage the skills and competencies required of personnel. Regularly verify that personnel have the competencies to fulfill their roles on the basis of their education, training, and/or experience and verify that these competencies are being maintained. APO07.04 Evaluate employee job performance — Conduct timely performance evaluations on a regular basis against individual and organizational goals and enterprise-approved standards. APO07.05 Plan and track the usage of IT and business human resources — Understand and track the current and future demand for business and IT human resources. APO07.06 Manage contract staff — Ensure that consultants and contract personnel who support the enterprise with I&T skills know and comply with the organization's policies and meet agreed-on contractual requirements.
evidence_requirements: IT staffing plans and skills inventory
Training and development programs and completion records
Performance evaluation records and competency assessments
Succession planning documentation for key IT roles

controls.title: Managed Relationships
controls.description: Manage the relationship between the business and IT in a formalized and transparent way that ensures a focus on achieving a common and shared goal of successful enterprise outcomes in support of strategic goals.
controls.full_text: APO08 — Managed Relationships. Purpose: Create improved outcomes, increased confidence, trust, and effective use of resources. Practices include: APO08.01 Understand business expectations — Understand the current business issues, objectives, and expectations for I&T. Ensure that requirements are understood, managed, and communicated. APO08.02 Identify opportunities, risk, and constraints for I&T to enhance the business — Identify potential opportunities for IT to enable and enhance the business, influence enterprise strategy, and contribute to improved business processes. APO08.03 Manage the business relationship — Manage the relationship with business stakeholders. Ensure that relationship roles and responsibilities are defined and assigned. APO08.04 Coordinate and communicate — Work with relevant stakeholders and coordinate, align, and communicate I&T value, performance, risk, and concerns. APO08.05 Provide input to the continual improvement of services — Continually improve and evolve I&T-enabled services and service delivery to the enterprise to align with changing enterprise requirements.
evidence_requirements: Business relationship management framework documentation
IT-business alignment meeting minutes and action items
Service level agreements and customer satisfaction reports
Business requirements documentation and prioritization records

controls.title: Managed Service Agreements
controls.description: Align I&T-enabled services and service levels with enterprise needs and expectations, including identification, specification, design, publishing, agreement, and monitoring of IT services, service levels, and performance indicators.
controls.full_text: APO09 — Managed Service Agreements. Purpose: Ensure that I&T services and service levels meet current and future enterprise needs. Practices include: APO09.01 Identify I&T services — Analyze business requirements and the degree to which I&T-enabling services and service levels support business processes. Discuss and agree with the business on potential service levels and compare against the current service portfolio. APO09.02 Catalog I&T-enabled services — Define and maintain one or more service catalogs for relevant target groups. Publish and maintain live I&T-enabled services in the service catalogs. APO09.03 Define and prepare service agreements — Define and prepare service agreements based on the options in the service catalogs. Include internal operational agreements. APO09.04 Monitor and report service levels — Monitor service levels, report on achievements, and identify trends. Provide the appropriate management information to aid performance management. APO09.05 Review service agreements and contracts — Conduct periodic reviews of service agreements and revise when necessary.
evidence_requirements: IT service catalog documentation
Service level agreements (SLAs) and operational level agreements (OLAs)
Service level performance monitoring reports
Service agreement review records and improvement actions

controls.title: Managed Vendors
controls.description: Manage I&T-related services provided by all types of vendors to meet enterprise requirements. This includes the selection of vendors, management of relationships, management of contracts, and reviewing and monitoring vendor performance.
controls.full_text: APO10 — Managed Vendors. Purpose: Optimize available I&T capabilities to support the I&T strategy and roadmap, minimize the risk associated with nonperforming or noncompliant vendors, and ensure competitive pricing. Practices include: APO10.01 Identify and evaluate vendor relationships and contracts — Identify and evaluate vendors and contracts to determine the right level of investment and oversight. Ensure compliance with agreements. APO10.02 Select vendors — Select vendors according to a fair and formal practice to ensure a viable best fit based on specified requirements. APO10.03 Manage vendor relationships and contracts — Formalize and manage the vendor relationship, including leveraging and enforcing existing contractual arrangements. APO10.04 Manage vendor risk — Identify and manage risks relating to vendors' ability to continually provide secure, efficient, and effective service delivery. APO10.05 Monitor vendor performance and compliance — Periodically review the overall vendor performance, compliance with agreement requirements, and value for money, and address identified issues.
evidence_requirements: Vendor selection criteria and evaluation documentation
Vendor contracts and service level agreements
Vendor risk assessment reports and due diligence records
Vendor performance monitoring reports and scorecards

controls.title: Managed Quality
controls.description: Define and communicate quality requirements in all processes, procedures, and the related enterprise outcomes. Monitor and measure quality against stated objectives and take necessary action when quality requirements are not being met.
controls.full_text: APO11 — Managed Quality. Purpose: Ensure consistent delivery of technology solutions and services to meet the quality requirements of the enterprise and satisfy stakeholder needs. Practices include: APO11.01 Establish a quality management system (QMS) — Establish and maintain a QMS that provides a standard, formal, and continuous approach to quality management for information, technology, and business processes that is aligned with business requirements and enterprise I&T management. APO11.02 Define and manage quality standards, practices, and procedures — Identify and maintain standards, procedures, and practices for key I&T processes to guide the enterprise in meeting the intent of the QMS. APO11.03 Focus quality management on customers — Focus quality management on customers by determining their requirements and ensuring alignment with the quality management practices. APO11.04 Perform quality monitoring, control, and reviews — Monitor the quality of processes and services on an ongoing basis as defined by the QMS. APO11.05 Integrate quality management into solutions for development and service delivery — Incorporate relevant quality management practices into the design, implementation, and monitoring of solutions development and service delivery. APO11.06 Maintain continuous improvement — Maintain and regularly communicate an overall quality plan that promotes continuous improvement.
evidence_requirements: Quality management system (QMS) documentation and policies
Quality standards and procedures for IT processes
Quality monitoring reports and defect tracking records
Continuous improvement plans and corrective action reports

controls.title: Managed Risk
controls.description: Continually identify, assess, and reduce I&T-related risk within levels of tolerance set by enterprise management. Integrate the management of I&T-related enterprise risk with overall enterprise risk management (ERM).
controls.full_text: APO12 — Managed Risk. Purpose: Integrate the management of I&T-related enterprise risk with overall ERM, and balance the costs and benefits of managing I&T-related enterprise risk. Practices include: APO12.01 Collect data — Identify and collect relevant data to enable effective I&T-related risk identification, analysis, and reporting. APO12.02 Analyze risk — Develop useful information to support risk decisions that take into account the business relevance of risk factors. APO12.03 Maintain a risk profile — Maintain an inventory of known risks and risk attributes, including expected frequency, potential impact, and responses, and of related resources, capabilities, and current control activities. APO12.04 Articulate risk — Provide information on the current state of I&T-related exposures and opportunities in a timely manner to all required stakeholders for appropriate response. APO12.05 Define a risk management action portfolio — Manage opportunities and reduce risk to an acceptable level as a portfolio. APO12.06 Respond to risk — Respond in a timely manner to risk events with effective measures to limit the magnitude of loss.
evidence_requirements: IT risk management policy and framework
IT risk register with risk assessments and treatment plans
Risk analysis reports with likelihood and impact assessments
Risk response actions and monitoring reports

controls.title: Managed Security
controls.description: Define, operate, and monitor a system for information security management. Keep the impact and occurrence of information security incidents within the enterprise's levels of risk appetite.
controls.full_text: APO13 — Managed Security. Purpose: Keep the impact and occurrence of information security incidents within the enterprise's risk appetite levels. Practices include: APO13.01 Establish and maintain an information security management system (ISMS) — Establish and maintain an ISMS that provides a standard, formal, and continuous approach to security management for information, software, and hardware. Address people, process, and technology security through a policy-driven approach that is aligned with business requirements and enterprise IT management. APO13.02 Define and manage an information security risk treatment plan — Maintain an information security plan that describes how information security risk is to be managed and aligned with the enterprise strategy and enterprise architecture. Ensure that recommendations for implementing security improvements are based on approved business cases. APO13.03 Monitor and review the ISMS — Maintain and regularly communicate the need for, and benefits of, continuous information security improvement. Collect and analyze data about the ISMS, and improve the effectiveness of the ISMS. Correct nonconformities to prevent recurrence. Promote a culture of security and continual improvement.
evidence_requirements: Information Security Management System (ISMS) documentation
Information security policies and standards
Security risk treatment plans and implementation evidence
ISMS monitoring reports and security metrics dashboards

controls.title: Managed Data
controls.description: Achieve and sustain effective management of the critical data assets required to support business processes. Ensure that data managed by the enterprise is reliable, of high quality, and available when needed.
controls.full_text: APO14 — Managed Data. Purpose: Ensure the effective utilization of the critical data assets to achieve enterprise goals and objectives. Ensure the quality of data available to support business decision making. Practices include: APO14.01 Define and maintain a data management framework — Define a data management framework that covers the enterprise scope and includes a data management strategy, data governance organization, data quality management, and data lifecycle management. APO14.02 Manage data profiles and metadata — Define standard data definitions, architectures, and data models that integrate and formalize fragmented data assets. APO14.03 Establish data quality management — Define, deploy, and maintain a data quality strategy. Define rules for data validation, cleansing, and enrichment. APO14.04 Manage data availability — Ensure that appropriate data is available when and where it is required by business processes. APO14.05 Manage data sharing and usage — Define and enforce rules and guidelines for data sharing and usage. APO14.06 Manage data retention and archiving — Define and implement a data retention and archival strategy. APO14.07 Manage data access and privacy — Ensure proper access control and privacy protection for data. APO14.08 Manage data recovery — Implement backup and recovery mechanisms to ensure data can be restored when needed.
evidence_requirements: Data governance framework and policies
Data quality management reports and metrics
Data classification and metadata management documentation
Data retention policies and backup/recovery procedures

controls.title: Managed Programs
controls.description: Manage all programs from the investment portfolio in coordination with the enterprise strategy and in a coordinated way based on the standard program management approach. Initiate, plan, and control the execution of programs and monitor expected outcomes.
controls.full_text: BAI01 — Managed Programs. Purpose: Realize the desired business value and reduce the risk of unexpected delays, costs, and value erosion. To do so, improve communications to and involvement of business and end users, ensure the value and quality of project deliverables, and maximize the contribution of programs to the investment portfolio. Practices include: BAI01.01 Maintain a standard approach for program and project management — Maintain a standard approach for program and project management that enables governance and management review, decision making, and delivery management activities to be focused consistently on the enterprise's value and goals. BAI01.02 Initiate a program — Initiate a program to confirm the expected benefits and obtain authorization to proceed. This includes agreeing on program sponsorship, confirming the program mandate through alignment with enterprise strategic objectives, and developing a program concept business case. BAI01.03 Manage stakeholder engagement — Manage stakeholder engagement to ensure an active exchange of accurate, consistent, and timely information that reaches all relevant stakeholders. BAI01.04 Develop and maintain the program plan — Formulate a program plan that shows program phases, milestones, activities, and resources. BAI01.05 Launch and execute the program — Launch and execute the program, and manage the program activities as required to deliver the expected benefits. BAI01.06 Monitor, control, and report on the program outcomes — Monitor and control the program as a whole, including meeting the specific benefit realization, delivery, and financial performance criteria.
evidence_requirements: Program management methodology and standards documentation
Program charters, business cases, and authorization records
Program plans with milestones, timelines, and resource allocations
Program status reports and benefits realization tracking

controls.title: Managed Requirements Definition
controls.description: Identify solutions and analyze requirements before acquisition or creation to ensure that they are in line with enterprise strategic requirements covering business processes, applications, information/data, infrastructure, and services.
controls.full_text: BAI02 — Managed Requirements Definition. Purpose: Create optimal solutions that meet enterprise needs while minimizing risk. Practices include: BAI02.01 Define and maintain business functional and technical requirements — Define and maintain business functional and technical requirements based on the prioritized requirements of the investment portfolio. Identify, prioritize, and agree on business information, functional, technical, and control requirements covering the scope of all initiatives required to achieve the expected outcomes of the proposed I&T-enabled business solution. BAI02.02 Perform a feasibility study and formulate alternative solutions — Perform a feasibility study of potential alternative solutions, assessing their viability and selecting the preferred option. BAI02.03 Manage requirements risk — Identify, document, mitigate, and control risk associated with the business requirements and the requirements definition process. BAI02.04 Obtain approval of requirements and solutions — Coordinate feedback from affected stakeholders and, at predefined key stages, obtain business sponsor or product owner approval and sign-off on functional and technical requirements and feasibility study reports.
evidence_requirements: Business requirements documentation and traceability matrices
Feasibility study reports and solution option analyses
Requirements sign-off and approval records
Requirements risk assessment documentation

controls.title: Managed Solutions Identification and Build
controls.description: Establish and maintain identified solutions in line with enterprise requirements covering design, development, procurement, and partnering with suppliers/vendors. Manage configuration, test preparation, testing, requirements management, and maintenance of business processes, applications, data/information, infrastructure, and services.
controls.full_text: BAI03 — Managed Solutions Identification and Build. Purpose: Establish timely and cost-effective solutions capable of supporting enterprise strategic and operational objectives. Practices include: BAI03.01 Design high-level solutions — Develop high-level design specifications based on business requirements, incorporating enterprise architecture direction and the technology strategy. BAI03.02 Design detailed solution components — Develop, document, and elaborate detailed solutions, aligning with enterprise architecture. BAI03.03 Develop solution components — Develop solution components in accordance with detailed design specifications following development methods and documentation standards, quality assurance (QA) requirements, and approval standards. BAI03.04 Procure solution components — Procure solution components based on the acquisition plan, in accordance with requirements and detailed design specifications. BAI03.05 Build solutions — Install and configure solutions and integrate with business process activities. Implement control, security, and auditability measures during configuration and during integration of hardware and infrastructural software. BAI03.06 Perform quality assurance — Develop, resource, and execute a QA plan aligned with the QMS to obtain the quality specified in the requirements definition and the enterprise's quality policies and procedures. BAI03.07 Prepare for solution testing — Establish a test plan and required environments to test the individual and integrated solution components. BAI03.08 Execute solution testing — Execute testing continually during development, including control testing, in accordance with the defined test plan and development practices in the appropriate environment. BAI03.09 Manage changes to requirements — Track the status of individual requirements and manage changes to requirements. BAI03.10 Maintain solutions — Develop and execute a plan for the maintenance of solution and infrastructure components.
evidence_requirements: Solution design documents (high-level and detailed)
Development standards and quality assurance test plans
Test results and defect tracking records
Solution procurement and build approval documentation

controls.title: Managed Availability and Capacity
controls.description: Balance current and future needs for availability, performance, and capacity with cost-effective service provision. Include assessment of current capabilities, forecasting of future needs based on business requirements, analysis of business impacts on resources, and assessment of technology and emerging standards.
controls.full_text: BAI04 — Managed Availability and Capacity. Purpose: Maintain service availability, efficient management of resources, and optimization of system performance through prediction of future performance and capacity requirements. Practices include: BAI04.01 Assess current availability, performance, and capacity and create a baseline — Assess availability, performance, and capacity of services and resources to ensure that cost-justifiable capacity and performance are available to support the business needs and deliver against SLAs. Create availability, performance, and capacity baselines. BAI04.02 Assess business impact — Identify important services to the enterprise, map services and resources to business processes, and identify business dependencies. Ensure that impact of nonavailability of resources is fully acknowledged in business terms. BAI04.03 Plan for new or changed service requirements — Plan and prioritize the availability and capacity implications of changing business needs and service requirements. BAI04.04 Monitor and review availability and capacity — Monitor, measure, analyze, report, and review availability, performance, and capacity. Identify deviations from established baselines. Review trend analysis reports identifying any significant issues and variances. Initiate actions where needed and ensure all outstanding issues are followed up. BAI04.05 Investigate and address availability, performance, and capacity issues — Address deviations by investigating and resolving identified availability, performance, and capacity issues.
evidence_requirements: Availability and capacity management plans
Performance baseline measurements and monitoring reports
Capacity forecasting and planning documentation
Business impact analysis for availability requirements

controls.title: Managed Organizational Change
controls.description: Maximize the likelihood of successfully implementing sustainable enterprise-wide organizational change quickly and with reduced risk, covering the complete life cycle of the change and all affected stakeholders in the business and IT.
controls.full_text: BAI05 — Managed Organizational Change. Purpose: Prepare and commit stakeholders for business change and reduce the risk of failure. Practices include: BAI05.01 Establish the desire to change — Understand the scope and impact of the envisioned change. Assess readiness and willingness for change amongst the affected stakeholders and throughout the enterprise. BAI05.02 Form an effective implementation team — Establish an effective implementation team by assembling appropriate members, creating trust, and establishing common goals and effectiveness measures. BAI05.03 Communicate desired vision — Communicate the desired vision for the change throughout the change domain. BAI05.04 Empower role players and identify short-term wins — Empower those with implementation roles and identify short-term wins. BAI05.05 Enable operation and use — Plan and implement all technical, operational, and usage aspects of the change so all affected parties can exercise their responsibility. BAI05.06 Embed new approaches — Embed new approaches by tracking implemented changes and assessing the effectiveness of the change plan. Take corrective action as needed. BAI05.07 Sustain changes — Sustain changes through effective training, ongoing communication, continuing executive commitment, monitoring of adoption, and sharing of lessons learned.
evidence_requirements: Organizational change management plans and strategies
Change readiness assessment reports
Change communication plans and stakeholder engagement records
Post-change effectiveness reviews and adoption metrics

controls.title: Managed IT Changes
controls.description: Manage all changes in a controlled manner, including standard changes and emergency maintenance relating to business processes, applications, and infrastructure. This includes change standards and procedures, impact assessment, prioritization, and authorization.
controls.full_text: BAI06 — Managed IT Changes. Purpose: Enable fast and reliable delivery of change to the business. Mitigate the risks of negatively impacting the stability or integrity of the changed environment. Practices include: BAI06.01 Evaluate, prioritize, and authorize change requests — Evaluate all requests for change to determine the impact on business processes and IT services, and to assess whether change will adversely affect the operational environment and introduce unacceptable risk. Ensure that changes are logged, prioritized, categorized, assessed, authorized, planned, and scheduled. BAI06.02 Manage emergency changes — Carefully manage emergency changes to minimize further incidents and make sure that the change is controlled and takes place securely. Verify that emergency changes are appropriately assessed and authorized after the change. BAI06.03 Track and report change status — Maintain a tracking and reporting system for all change requests, including rejected changes. Make status reporting available as an input to performance and governance reporting. BAI06.04 Close and document the changes — Whenever changes are implemented, update accordingly the solution and user documentation and the procedures affected by the change.
evidence_requirements: Change management policy and procedures
Change request logs with assessment, authorization, and implementation records
Change Advisory Board (CAB) meeting minutes and decisions
Post-implementation review reports and emergency change documentation

controls.title: Managed IT Change Acceptance and Transitioning
controls.description: Formally accept and make operational new solutions, including implementation planning, system and data conversion, acceptance testing, communication, release preparation, promotion to production, early production support, and a post-implementation review.
controls.full_text: BAI07 — Managed IT Change Acceptance and Transitioning. Purpose: Implement solutions safely and in line with the agreed-on expectations and outcomes. Practices include: BAI07.01 Establish an implementation plan — Establish an implementation plan that covers system and data conversion, acceptance testing criteria, communication, training, release preparation, promotion to production, early production support, a fallback/back-out plan, and a post-implementation review. APO07.02 Plan business process, system, and data conversion — Prepare for business process, IT service, and infrastructure migration, including data/system migration and creation/revision of process documentation, training materials, etc. BAI07.03 Plan acceptance tests — Establish a test plan based on enterprise standards that defines roles, responsibilities, and entry and exit criteria. Ensure that the plan is approved by relevant parties. BAI07.04 Establish a test environment — Define and establish a secure test environment representative of the planned business process and IT operations environment relative to security, internal controls, operational practices, data quality and privacy requirements, and workloads. BAI07.05 Perform acceptance tests — Test changes independently in accordance with the defined test plan prior to migration to the live operational environment. BAI07.06 Promote to production and manage releases — After testing, control the handover of the changed system to operations, keeping it in line with the implementation plan. Where applicable, run the old and new systems in parallel for a defined period to compare behavior and results. BAI07.07 Provide early production support — Provide early production support by resolving operational issues during the initial period after change. BAI07.08 Perform a post-implementation review — Perform a post-implementation review to confirm outcomes and identify lessons learned.
evidence_requirements: Release and deployment plans with rollback procedures
User acceptance testing (UAT) results and sign-off documentation
Production promotion checklists and authorization records
Post-implementation review reports and lessons learned

controls.title: Managed Knowledge
controls.description: Maintain the availability of relevant, current, validated, and reliable knowledge and information to support all process activities and to facilitate decision making. Plan for the identification, gathering, organizing, maintaining, use, and retirement of knowledge.
controls.full_text: BAI08 — Managed Knowledge. Purpose: Provide the knowledge required to support all staff in their work activities and for informed decision making and enhanced productivity. Practices include: BAI08.01 Nurture and facilitate a knowledge-sharing culture — Design and implement a scheme to nurture and facilitate a knowledge-sharing culture. BAI08.02 Identify and classify sources of information — Identify, validate, and classify diverse sources of internal and external information required for enabling the effective use of knowledge, including organization knowledge, process documentation, industry information, partner information, and competitive intelligence. BAI08.03 Organize and contextualize information into knowledge — Organize information based on quality criteria and apply knowledge management principles to identify and classify related information as knowledge. BAI08.04 Use and share knowledge — Propagate available knowledge resources to relevant stakeholders and communicate how those resources can be used to address different needs. BAI08.05 Evaluate and update or retire information — Measure the use and evaluate the currency and relevance of knowledge. Update or retire outdated knowledge.
evidence_requirements: Knowledge management strategy and policies
Knowledge repository/wiki with maintained documentation
Knowledge sharing activity records and training materials

controls.title: Managed Assets
controls.description: Manage I&T assets through their life cycle to make sure that their use delivers value at optimal cost, they remain operational (fit for purpose), they are accounted for, and they are physically protected.
controls.full_text: BAI09 — Managed Assets. Purpose: Account for all I&T assets and optimize the value provided by these assets. Practices include: BAI09.01 Identify and record current assets — Maintain an up-to-date and accurate record of all I&T assets required to deliver services and ensure alignment with configuration management and financial management. BAI09.02 Manage critical assets — Identify assets that are critical in providing service capability and take steps to maximize their reliability and availability to support business needs. BAI09.03 Manage the asset life cycle — Manage assets from procurement to disposal to ensure that they are utilized as effectively and efficiently as possible and are accounted for and physically protected. BAI09.04 Optimize asset costs — Regularly review the overall asset base to identify ways to optimize costs and maintain alignment with business needs. BAI09.05 Manage licenses — Manage licenses to ensure that the optimal number is procured, retained, and deployed in relation to required business usage, and the software installed is in compliance with license agreements.
evidence_requirements: IT asset inventory and register with lifecycle tracking
Critical asset identification and protection measures
Software license management records and compliance reports
Asset disposal and decommissioning procedures and records

controls.title: Managed Configuration
controls.description: Define and maintain descriptions and relationships of key resources and capabilities required to deliver I&T-enabled services, including collecting configuration information, establishing baselines, verifying and auditing configuration information, and updating the configuration repository.
controls.full_text: BAI10 — Managed Configuration. Purpose: Provide sufficient information about service assets to enable the service to be effectively managed, assess the impact of changes, and deal with service incidents. Practices include: BAI10.01 Establish and maintain a configuration model — Establish and maintain a logical model of the services, assets, and infrastructure and how to record configuration items (CIs) and the relationships among them. Include the CIs considered necessary to manage services effectively and to provide a single, reliable description of the assets in a service. BAI10.02 Establish and maintain a configuration repository and baseline — Establish and maintain a configuration management repository and create controlled configuration baselines. BAI10.03 Maintain and control configuration items — Maintain an up-to-date repository of configuration items by populating with changes. BAI10.04 Produce status and configuration reports — Define and produce configuration reports on status changes of configuration items. BAI10.05 Verify and review integrity of the configuration repository — Periodically review the configuration repository and verify completeness and correctness against the desired target.
evidence_requirements: Configuration management database (CMDB) documentation
Configuration baselines and controlled CI records
Configuration audit and verification reports
Configuration status and change tracking reports

controls.title: Managed Projects
controls.description: Manage all projects from the investment portfolio in alignment with enterprise strategy and in a coordinated way based on the standard project management approach. Initiate, plan, control, and execute projects, and close with a post-implementation review.
controls.full_text: BAI11 — Managed Projects. Purpose: Realize business value from I&T-enabled projects and reduce the risk of unexpected delays, costs, and value erosion by improving communication to and involvement of business and end users, ensuring the value and quality of project deliverables, and maximizing their contribution to the defined investment and services portfolios. Practices include: BAI11.01 Maintain a standard approach for project management — Maintain a standard approach for project management. BAI11.02 Start up and initiate a project — Define and document the nature and scope of the project to confirm and develop among stakeholders a common understanding of project scope. BAI11.03 Manage stakeholder engagement — Manage stakeholder engagement to ensure an active exchange of accurate, consistent, and timely information. BAI11.04 Develop and maintain the project plan — Establish a formal, approved project plan (covering business and IT resources) to guide project execution and control throughout the life of the project. BAI11.05 Manage project quality — Prepare and execute a quality management plan, processes, and practices aligned with the QMS. BAI11.06 Manage project risk — Eliminate or minimize specific risks associated with the project through a systematic process of planning, identifying, analyzing, responding to, and monitoring and controlling the areas or events that have the potential to cause unwanted change. BAI11.07 Monitor and control a project — Measure project performance against key project performance criteria such as schedule, quality, cost, and risk. Identify any deviations from the plan. BAI11.08 Manage project resources — Determine and allocate all required project resources, including people, finance, and infrastructure, and use them effectively and efficiently. BAI11.09 Close a project or iteration — At the end of each project, iteration, or release, require project stakeholders to ascertain whether the project delivered the planned results and value. Identify and communicate outstanding activities and lessons learned.
evidence_requirements: Project management methodology and standards
Project charters, plans, and stakeholder engagement documentation
Project status reports with schedule, cost, and risk tracking
Project closure reports with lessons learned and benefits assessment

controls.title: Managed Operations
controls.description: Coordinate and execute the activities and operational procedures required to deliver internal and outsourced IT services, including the execution of predefined standard operating procedures and the required monitoring activities.
controls.full_text: DSS01 — Managed Operations. Purpose: Deliver I&T operational product and service outcomes as planned. Practices include: DSS01.01 Perform operational procedures — Perform operational procedures, including but not limited to job scheduling, protection of sensitive output, and infrastructure monitoring and maintenance. Maintain an operational schedule that reflects the requirements. Ensure that operational standards are applied consistently. DSS01.02 Manage outsourced IT services — Manage the operation of outsourced IT services to maintain the protection of enterprise information and the reliability of service delivery. DSS01.03 Monitor IT infrastructure — Monitor the IT infrastructure and related events. Store sufficient chronological information in operations logs to enable the reconstruction, review, and examination of the time sequences of operations and the other activities surrounding or supporting operations. DSS01.04 Manage the environment — Maintain measures for protection against environmental factors. Install specialized equipment and devices to monitor and control the environment. DSS01.05 Manage facilities — Manage facilities, including power and communications equipment, in line with laws and regulations, technical and business requirements, vendor specifications, and health and safety guidelines.
evidence_requirements: Standard operating procedures (SOPs) for IT operations
Operations monitoring logs and infrastructure event records
Facilities and environmental management records
Outsourced service management reports and compliance evidence

controls.title: Managed Service Requests and Incidents
controls.description: Provide timely and effective response to user requests and resolution of all types of incidents. Restore normal service; record and fulfill user requests; and record, investigate, diagnose, escalate, and resolve incidents.
controls.full_text: DSS02 — Managed Service Requests and Incidents. Purpose: Achieve increased productivity and minimize disruptions through quick resolution of user queries and incidents. Assess the impact of changes and deal with service incidents. Resolve user requests and restore service in response to incidents. Practices include: DSS02.01 Define classification schemes for incidents and service requests — Define classification and prioritization schemes for incidents and service requests, and criteria for problem registration to ensure consistent approaches. DSS02.02 Record, classify, and prioritize requests and incidents — Identify, record, and classify service requests and incidents and assign a priority according to business criticality and service agreements. DSS02.03 Verify, approve, and fulfill service requests — Select the appropriate request procedures and verify that the service requests fulfill the defined request criteria. Obtain approval, if required, and fulfill the requests. DSS02.04 Investigate, diagnose, and allocate incidents — Identify and record incident symptoms, determine possible causes, and allocate for resolution. DSS02.05 Resolve and recover from incidents — Document, apply, and test the identified solutions or workarounds and perform recovery actions to restore the I&T-related service. DSS02.06 Close service requests and incidents — Verify satisfactory incident resolution and/or satisfactory request fulfillment, and close. DSS02.07 Track status and produce reports — Regularly track, analyze, and report incident and request fulfillment trends to provide information for continual improvement.
evidence_requirements: Incident and service request management procedures
Incident ticket logs with classification, prioritization, and resolution details
Incident trend analysis and reporting dashboards
Service request fulfillment records and SLA compliance reports

controls.title: Managed Problems
controls.description: Identify and classify problems and their root causes and provide timely resolution to prevent recurring incidents. Provide recommendations for improvements.
controls.full_text: DSS03 — Managed Problems. Purpose: Increase availability, improve service levels, reduce costs, and improve customer convenience and satisfaction by reducing the number of operational problems and identify root causes as part of problem resolution. Practices include: DSS03.01 Identify and classify problems — Define and implement criteria and procedures to report problems identified including problem classification, categorization, and prioritization. DSS03.02 Investigate and diagnose problems — Investigate and diagnose problems using relevant subject matter experts to assess and analyze root causes. DSS03.03 Raise known errors — As soon as root causes of problems are identified, create known error records, document workarounds, and identify potential solutions. DSS03.04 Resolve and close problems — Identify and initiate sustainable solutions addressing the root cause, raising change requests via the established change management process if required to resolve errors. Ensure that the personnel affected are aware of the actions taken and the plans developed for preventing future incidents from occurring. DSS03.05 Perform proactive problem management — Collect and analyze operational data (especially incident and change records) to identify emerging trends that may indicate problems. Log problem records to enable assessment.
evidence_requirements: Problem management procedures and classification criteria
Problem records with root cause analysis documentation
Known error database and workaround documentation
Proactive problem management trend analysis reports

controls.title: Managed Continuity
controls.description: Establish and maintain a plan to enable the business and IT to respond to incidents and disruptions in order to continue operation of critical business processes and required IT services and maintain availability of information at a level acceptable to the enterprise.
controls.full_text: DSS04 — Managed Continuity. Purpose: Adapt rapidly, continue business operations, and maintain availability of resources and information at a level acceptable to the enterprise in the event of a significant disruption (e.g., threats, opportunities, demands). Practices include: DSS04.01 Define the business continuity policy, objectives, and scope — Define the business continuity policy and scope, aligned with enterprise and stakeholder objectives. DSS04.02 Maintain a continuity strategy — Evaluate business continuity management options and choose a cost-effective and viable continuity strategy that will ensure enterprise recovery and continuity in the face of a disaster or other major incident or disruption. DSS04.03 Develop and implement a business continuity response — Develop a business continuity plan (BCP) based on the strategy that documents the procedures and information in readiness for use in an incident to enable the enterprise to continue its critical activities. DSS04.04 Exercise, test, and review the BCP — Test the continuity arrangements on a regular basis to exercise the recovery plans against predetermined outcomes and to allow innovative solutions to be developed and help to verify over time that the plan will work as anticipated. DSS04.05 Review, maintain, and improve the continuity plan — Conduct a management review of the continuity capability at regular intervals to ensure its continued suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness. Manage changes to the plan in accordance with the change control process. DSS04.06 Conduct continuity plan training — Provide all concerned internal and external parties with regular training sessions regarding the procedures and their roles and responsibilities in case of an incident or disaster. DSS04.07 Manage backup arrangements — Maintain availability of business-critical information. DSS04.08 Conduct post-resumption review — Assess the adequacy of the BCP following the successful resumption of business processes and services after a disruption.
evidence_requirements: Business continuity policy and strategy documentation
Business continuity plans (BCPs) and disaster recovery plans (DRPs)
BCP test and exercise results with lessons learned
Backup and recovery procedures and verification records

controls.title: Managed Security Services
controls.description: Protect enterprise information to maintain the level of information security risk acceptable to the enterprise in accordance with the security policy. Establish and maintain information security roles and access privileges and perform security monitoring.
controls.full_text: DSS05 — Managed Security Services. Purpose: Minimize the business impact of operational information security vulnerabilities and incidents. Practices include: DSS05.01 Protect against malware — Implement and maintain preventive, detective, and corrective measures in place (especially up-to-date security patches and virus control) across the enterprise to protect information systems and technology from malware. DSS05.02 Manage network and connectivity security — Use security measures and related management procedures to protect information over all methods of connectivity. DSS05.03 Manage endpoint security — Ensure that endpoints (e.g., laptop, desktop, server, and other mobile and network devices or software) are secured at a level that is equal to or greater than the defined security requirements of the information processed, stored, or transmitted. DSS05.04 Manage user identity and logical access — Ensure that all users have information access rights in accordance with their business requirements and coordinate with business units that manage their own access rights within business processes. DSS05.05 Manage physical access to I&T assets — Define and implement procedures to grant, limit, and revoke access to premises, buildings, and areas according to business needs, including emergencies. Access to premises, buildings, and areas should be justified, authorized, logged, and monitored. DSS05.06 Manage sensitive documents and output devices — Establish appropriate physical safeguards, accounting practices, and inventory management over sensitive IT assets, such as special forms, negotiable instruments, special-purpose printers, or security tokens. DSS05.07 Monitor the infrastructure for security-related events — Using intrusion detection tools, monitor the infrastructure for unauthorized access and ensure that any events are integrated with general event monitoring and incident management.
evidence_requirements: Security operations procedures and monitoring configurations
Malware protection deployment and update records
Access control lists and user access review documentation
Security event monitoring logs and incident detection reports

controls.title: Managed Business Process Controls
controls.description: Define and maintain appropriate business process controls to ensure that information related to and processed by in-house or outsourced business processes satisfies all relevant information control requirements.
controls.full_text: DSS06 — Managed Business Process Controls. Purpose: Maintain information integrity and the security of information assets handled within business processes in the enterprise or outsourced. Practices include: DSS06.01 Align control activities embedded in business processes with enterprise objectives — Continually assess and monitor the execution of the business process activities and related controls (based on enterprise risk) to ensure that the processing controls are aligned with business needs. DSS06.02 Control the processing of information — Operate the execution of the business process activities and related controls, based on enterprise risk, to ensure that information processing is valid, complete, accurate, timely, and secure (i.e., it reflects legitimate and authorized business use). DSS06.03 Manage roles, responsibilities, access privileges, and levels of authority — Manage the business roles, responsibilities, levels of authority, and segregation of duties needed to support the business process objectives. Authorize access to any information assets related to the business processes. DSS06.04 Manage errors and exceptions — Manage business process exceptions and errors, and facilitate their correction. Include escalation of business process errors and exceptions and the execution of defined corrective actions. DSS06.05 Ensure traceability and accountability for information events — Ensure that business information can be traced to the originating business event and accountable parties. DSS06.06 Secure information assets — Secure information assets accessible by the business through approved methods, including information in electronic form, information in physical form, and information during transit.
evidence_requirements: Business process control documentation and procedures
Segregation of duties matrix and access privilege records
Information processing validation and integrity check records
Error and exception handling logs with corrective action documentation

controls.title: Managed Performance and Conformance Monitoring
controls.description: Collect, validate, and evaluate business, IT, and process goals and metrics. Monitor that processes are performing against agreed-on performance and conformance goals and metrics, and provide reporting that is systematic and timely.
controls.full_text: MEA01 — Managed Performance and Conformance Monitoring. Purpose: Provide transparency of performance and conformance and drive achievement of goals. Practices include: MEA01.01 Establish a monitoring approach — Engage with stakeholders to establish and maintain a monitoring approach to define the objectives, scope, and method for measuring business solution and service delivery and the contribution of I&T. Integrate this approach with the enterprise performance management system. MEA01.02 Set performance and conformance targets — Work with stakeholders to define, periodically review, update, and approve performance and conformance targets within the performance measurement system. MEA01.03 Collect and process performance and conformance data — Collect and process timely and accurate data aligned with enterprise approaches. MEA01.04 Analyze and report performance — Periodically review and report performance against targets, using a method that provides a succinct, all-around view of I&T performance and fits within the enterprise monitoring system. MEA01.05 Ensure the implementation of corrective actions — Assist stakeholders in identifying, initiating, and tracking corrective actions to address anomalies.
evidence_requirements: Performance monitoring framework and KPI definitions
Performance dashboards and scorecards with trend analysis
Conformance monitoring reports and target achievement records
Corrective action plans and follow-up tracking documentation

controls.title: Managed System of Internal Control
controls.description: Continuously monitor and evaluate the control environment, including self-assessments and independent assurance reviews. Enable management to identify control deficiencies and inefficiencies and to initiate improvement actions.
controls.full_text: MEA02 — Managed System of Internal Control. Purpose: Obtain transparency for key stakeholders on the adequacy of the system of internal controls and thus provide trust in operations, confidence in the achievement of enterprise objectives, and an adequate understanding of residual risk. Practices include: MEA02.01 Monitor internal controls — Continuously monitor, benchmark, and improve the I&T control environment and control framework to meet organizational objectives. MEA02.02 Review business process controls effectiveness — Review the operation of controls, including a review of monitoring and test evidence, to ensure that controls within business processes operate effectively. Also include activities to maintain evidence of the effective operation of controls through mechanisms such as periodic testing of controls, continuous controls monitoring, independent assessments, command and control centers, and network operation centers. MEA02.03 Perform control self-assessments — Encourage management and process owners to take positive ownership of control improvement through a continuing program of self-assessment to evaluate the completeness and effectiveness of management's control over processes, policies, and contracts. MEA02.04 Identify and report control deficiencies — Identify control deficiencies and analyze and identify their underlying root causes. Escalate control deficiencies and report to stakeholders.
evidence_requirements: Internal control framework documentation and policies
Control self-assessment (CSA) reports and results
Internal control testing results and effectiveness reports
Control deficiency registers and remediation tracking

controls.title: Managed Compliance with External Requirements
controls.description: Evaluate that IT processes and IT-supported business processes are compliant with laws, regulations, and contractual requirements. Obtain assurance that the requirements have been identified and complied with, and integrate IT compliance with overall enterprise compliance.
controls.full_text: MEA03 — Managed Compliance with External Requirements. Purpose: Ensure that the enterprise is compliant with all applicable external requirements. Practices include: MEA03.01 Identify external compliance requirements — On a continuous basis, identify and monitor for changes in local and international laws, regulations, and other external requirements that must be complied with from an I&T perspective. MEA03.02 Optimize response to external requirements — Review and adjust policies, principles, standards, procedures, and methodologies to ensure that legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements are addressed and communicated. Consider industry standards, codes of good practice, and best practice guidance for adoption and adaptation. MEA03.03 Confirm external compliance — Confirm compliance of policies, principles, standards, procedures, and methodologies with legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements. MEA03.04 Obtain assurance of external compliance — Obtain and report assurance of compliance and adherence to policies, principles, standards, procedures, and methodologies. Confirm that corrective actions to address compliance gaps are closed in a timely manner.
evidence_requirements: Regulatory compliance register with applicable requirements
Compliance assessment reports and gap analyses
Evidence of compliance with laws, regulations, and contracts
External audit reports and regulatory examination findings

controls.title: Managed Assurance
controls.description: Enable the organization to design and develop efficient and effective assurance initiatives by providing guidance on planning, scoping, executing, and following up on assurance reviews, using a roadmap based on well-accepted assurance approaches.
controls.full_text: MEA04 — Managed Assurance. Purpose: Enable the organization to design and develop efficient and effective assurance initiatives by providing guidance on the planning, scoping, executing, and following up on assurance reviews, using a roadmap based on well-accepted assurance approaches. Practices include: MEA04.01 Plan assurance initiatives — Plan assurance initiatives based on enterprise objectives, conformance objectives, assurance objectives, and an enterprise and I&T risk-driven prioritization process and in alignment with applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations. MEA04.02 Scope assurance initiatives — Define the scope of assurance initiatives based on the assurance objectives, and consult relevant stakeholders. MEA04.03 Execute assurance initiatives — Execute the planned assurance initiatives. Report identified findings. Provide positive assurance opinions, where appropriate, and recommendations for improvement relating to identified operational performance, external compliance, and internal control system residual risks. MEA04.04 Communicate assurance results — Report the results of assurance initiatives to management and other relevant stakeholders. Enable management to take appropriate corrective action where needed. MEA04.05 Follow up on assurance recommendations — Identify, track, and report management actions that have been initiated in response to assurance findings and recommendations. Confirm implementation of recommendations.
evidence_requirements: Assurance initiative plans with scope and objectives
Assurance review reports with findings and recommendations
Management action plans responding to assurance findings
Follow-up tracking of assurance recommendation implementation
